title: Fridge deodoriser tabs
subtitle: Pressed bicarbonate of soda tabs with citric acid and lemon essential oil for open-air fridge use
excerpt: Bicarbonate of soda adsorbs odours from the air around it without adding any scent of its own. Formed into tabs with a small amount of citric acid and essential oil and left open in the fridge, these work far better than a loose bowl of powder. Replace monthly.
sourceNotes: Bicarbonate of soda deodoriser properties from standard chemistry references. Pressed tab method from natural cleaning and homemaking guides.

recipe.batchNotes: Double or triple for a larger batch. Store spare tabs in a sealed tin.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make a month's supply at a time.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months stored sealed. Once placed in the fridge, replace monthly. A tab that has absorbed fridge odours fully will no longer smell of citrus; that is the signal to replace it.

glossaryTerms.definition: Sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO3). A mild alkali that neutralises acidic odour molecules in the air around it. It does not mask odours but chemically neutralises them, making it genuinely effective as a deodoriser rather than a fragrance cover.

A weak organic acid naturally found in citrus fruit. In cleaning and deodorising tabs it reacts with bicarbonate of soda in the presence of moisture to create a gentle effervescent action that helps release the powder's surface area.
The binding of molecules (such as odour compounds) to a surface. Bicarbonate of soda adsorbs volatile odour molecules from the air onto its surface, neutralising them.

content.text: Bicarbonate of soda works as a fridge deodoriser through
: it binds acidic volatile compounds from food smells onto its surface and neutralises them.

into a pressed tab without glue or binders. A small amount of lemon or orange essential oil adds a mild freshness but the deodorising effect comes entirely from the bicarbonate. Replace each tab monthly. Shelf life of unused tabs is 12 months.

items.prepNote: 5% of formula; approximately 100 drops per 100 g

content.text: Combine bicarbonate of soda and citric acid in a bowl and stir briefly. Add the essential oil drop by drop, stirring constantly; adding too quickly will cause a fizzing reaction between the oil droplets and the citric acid. Once combined, the mixture should just hold together when pressed firmly between the fingers.

Press the mixture firmly into the silicone mould cavities, compacting as much as possible. Leave to set for at least 2 hours at room temperature before unmoulding. If the tabs crumble, add a few more drops of essential oil and repress.

Store unused tabs in a sealed tin or bag for up to 12 months. Place one tab open in the fridge on a small saucer and replace monthly. When the citrus scent has faded completely, the tab has done its job.
